tests.test_name: Appium - PO - single test, core output structure
tests.description: Verifies that a Product Owner uploading a single Appium test file receives a valid, fully resolved JSON documentation output containing at least one named test.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system produces visible output after analyzing the uploaded test file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All fields in the downloaded JSON are populated — no placeholders or missing data remain
At least one test entry is present in the output for a single uploaded test case

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each documented test is identifiable by a meaningful name

tests.test_name: Appium - PO - DataProvider expands one test per row
tests.description: Verifies that a Product Owner uploading a data-driven Appium test file sees the output expanded into one separate test entry per data row, yielding three or more documented tests.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system produces visible output after analyzing the data-driven test file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All fields in the downloaded JSON are fully populated with no placeholders
Data rows are each expanded into individual test entries rather than collapsed into one

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each expanded test variant is identifiable by a meaningful name

tests.test_name: Appium - QAM - single test, coverage matrix populated
tests.description: Verifies that a QA Manager uploading a single Appium test file receives a valid JSON output where each test includes a populated test coverage matrix.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system produces visible output after analyzing the uploaded test file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All fields in the downloaded JSON are fully populated with no placeholders
At least one test entry is present in the output

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each documented test is identifiable by a meaningful name

test_coverage.verification_step: First test has a non-empty test_coverage_matrix array
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The QA Manager view includes a coverage matrix with at least one entry, providing traceability detail not present in the Product Owner view

tests.description: Verifies that a QA Manager uploading a data-driven Appium test file sees the output collapsed into a single test entry whose coverage matrix rows represent each data example using the correct schema.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system produces visible output after analyzing the data-driven test file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All fields in the downloaded JSON are fully populated with no placeholders
Data-driven tests are collapsed into a single logical test entry in the QA Manager view, contrasting with the Product Owner expansion behavior

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The collapsed test entry is identifiable by a meaningful name

test_coverage.verification_step: Coverage matrix row count matches the examples
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each data row from the DataProvider is represented as a distinct row in the coverage matrix

test_coverage.verification_step: Coverage matrix rows use the correct schema for generic
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each coverage matrix row conforms to the expected data structure for generic-type test documentation
